#47Spirit announce lineups for three academy teams

10

1 reply

#85

ANGE1 | 

 texraupen

Nah, EG did something way more stupid. Because they just signed 3 rosters, all of them were not academies. And because of that the other 2 rosters couldn't play tournaments. Main EG was for big partnered stuff and qualifiers for big events, the other one was for smaller local events (think Fragadelphia, but without EU teams coming over because of VRS), and the last one had to play close to nothing because of CoI, so they ended up with basically only cash cups. Like, I hope the salary was good, because from competitive standpoint, those 2 other teams that were originally mix teams had better opportunities to qualify and compete as mix teams. Here it is basically something like U18, U16 and U12 (I don't know exact way they separate it, but it's based on age).

2026-03-10 07:46

Please log in and sync your account with Steam to upvote.

#477NAVI sign up for Parken Challenger Championship

 | 

 i_hate_weird_formats

What's with this trend of changing a player straight before the major? Surprise tactics?

2026-03-13 12:25

Please log in and sync your account with Steam to upvote.

0

#483

 | 

 Bouncy2370

not really, it's more that it's no longer enough time, i think, to handle tier 1 player transfer if you do it after the major. then everyone is going to be trying to do it

2026-03-13 12:31

Please log in and sync your account with Steam to upvote.

3

#485

 cs_source2

Because the Major invite date is a better timing to change players than player breaks now. After you have the invite secured, you have a couple months to practice without needing to worry about bad results at the start. Then you're also in a better spot to to get an invite to the following Major, as the events early in the season and before the next invite date will mostly decide it (whereas results between the Major invite date and the Major will have decayed massively already).

2026-03-13 12:41

Please log in and sync your account with Steam to upvote.

8

#512

 | 

 i_hate_weird_formats

Makes sense

2026-03-13 15:22

#678

ANGE1 | 

 texraupen

And so far Liquid are to blame for triggering different edge cases twice. One time they stole a spot from NA to EU and then back when it didn't really work (because core was in tact, but changed region, it's back when they their first EU adventure), and now this. Then there was Wildcard situation. Or how rules were changed to keep Complexity NA because 2-2-1, so now Sonic is "whatever region is the weakest among 2-2" basically And I mean, sure, don't blame the player, blame the game. #639

 | 

 TorontoCS

Lets be real here. Liquid were going NA whether you liked it or not. The EU roster was a sinking ship and Liquid has spent millions in the past two years on Roster moves for nothing. They were left with no choice. Missing the Major would've been the final nail in the coffin for Liquid just like what happened to Cloud9. Had Liquid been able to do this roster change in the player break, they would've and Liquid would've still beaten Marsborne(unless your delusional and believe they wouldn't have). The problem was the G2 was running with their roster to see what they can do and failed. So this is the best for BOTH teams and Liquid literally did nothing wrong lmao. Anyone that can't see that is just a solid, major hater of Liquid. There is no question of morality but of smart decisions. Something Liquid did after 1000 horrible decisions and TONS of roster changes spending millions upon millions to miss Majors like they did with Cadian and now finally did something right. Get over it and stop crying.

2026-03-13 19:22

Please log in and sync your account with Steam to upvote.

#646

 | 

 celtek

Nah fuck Liquid, I don't care if this was technically "the right move" -- like, no shit it's the right move for them. They aren't making the major unless they pivot to the NA region - because, let's face it, they fucking suck. And the best part about this is that Malbs isn't going to fix literally anything on this team. It's still going to be a bog water ass team with absolutely zero prospects of cracking top 10. This is a move that is literally designed to help them get a Major invite, and that's it. 6 months from now I wouldn't be surprised to see Liquid on the cusp of not making it to the major, again, because this roster is cooked. I can't wait to watch them get pooped on in stage 1 by some tier 5 faceit pug that wanders their way in to stage 1. Lmao.
